Binding.description:
Binding-expression string of the form `"{{...}}"`. Authors may
substitute any primitive value with a binding so the runtime
resolves the value at render time. Used as an alternative branch
in primitives that otherwise expect a specific structural shape
(AssetRef, Dimension, BorderRadius, Alignment, …).

BorderRadius.description:
Per-corner radius value. A bare number applies uniformly to all
four corners; the object form lets each corner take an independent
radius. `all` is a shorthand for setting every corner at once and
is overridden by individual corner keys.

Corner names are **directional** (RTL-aware) per Material 3:

- `topStart` — top corner on the leading edge (top-left in LTR,
  top-right in RTL).
- `topEnd` — top corner on the trailing edge.
- `bottomStart` / `bottomEnd` — same convention on the bottom row.

This is the cross-schema canonical for any "per-corner radius"
shape — referenced by `theme.Shape` (M3 shape token sizes) and by
the `BoxDecoration.borderRadius` widget property.

AssetRef.description:
Reference to an asset (image, font, audio, video, SVG, …). Five
scheme prefixes are accepted, picked by the runtime to dispatch
the right loader:

- `bundle://<path>` — asset packaged inside the active bundle.
- `https://<url>` / `http://<url>` — fetched over the network and
  cached on first render.
- `data:<mime>;base64,<payload>` (or `data:<mime>,<urlencoded>`) —
  inline bytes carried in the DSL itself.
- `assets/<path>` — Flutter-style local asset path declared in the
  consumer app's pubspec asset list (used by host integrations that
  ship icon SVGs alongside the runtime).
- `client://<channel>/<id>` — resource served by the client / host
  process via an MCP client-extension channel.

Used cross-schema by `app` (splash · publisher logos · font
assets), `page`/widgets (image · icon · BackgroundImage · video
src), and `theme` (font asset registration). Lives under
`configs/_primitive/` for that reason.

Dimension.description:
Numeric size — accepts a plain `number` (logical pixels) OR the
legacy MCP UI DSL v1.0 `{value, unit}` object form. The `unit`
field is informational; the runtime treats every dimension as
logical pixels.

Alignment.description:
2D alignment within a rectangle. Token names are **directional**
(RTL-aware) per Material 3:

- `topStart` — top corner on the leading edge (top-left in LTR,
  top-right in RTL).
- `topEnd` — top corner on the trailing edge.
- `centerStart` / `centerEnd`, `bottomStart` / `bottomEnd` — same
  convention on the middle / bottom rows.
- `topCenter`, `center`, `bottomCenter` — neutral on the cross
  axis.

Object form `{x, y}` is a numeric pair where each axis runs from
-1 (start) through 0 (center) to 1 (end). The numeric form
supports any point inside or outside the rectangle.

Cross-schema canonical for any "2D rectangle alignment" use —
child placement (`box.alignment`, `align.alignment`,
`stack.alignment`), gradient endpoints (`Gradient.begin/end`),
image fit (`BackgroundImage.alignment`).

AnimationCurve.description:
Easing curve applied to an animation's progress. Eleven canonical
values cover the M3-recommended palette plus the CSS-familiar
basics:

- **CSS-familiar** — `linear`, `easeIn`, `easeOut`, `easeInOut`.
- **M3 standard** — `standard`, `standardAccelerate`,
  `standardDecelerate`. Used for everyday transitions; minimal
  overshoot, clean cubic.
- **M3 emphasized** — `emphasized`, `emphasizedAccelerate`,
  `emphasizedDecelerate`. Used for hero / page transitions; more
  dramatic acceleration profile.
- **Bounce** — `bounceIn`, `bounceOut`. For attention / alert
  micro-interactions.

Cross-schema canonical for `animatedContainer.curve`,
`animatedOpacity.curve`, RouteTransition, scrollAnimated easing,
and any future implicit-animation widget. Matches the M3 motion
spec's named tokens 1:1 so theme-level motion overrides stay
vendor-neutral.

Gradient.description:
Multi-stop gradient fill. Selected by `type`:

- `linear` — straight axis from `begin` (default `centerLeft`) to
  `end` (default `centerRight`).
- `radial` — circular gradient out from `center` (default `center`)
  to `radius` (default `0.5`, expressed as a fraction of the
  bounding rectangle's longest side).
- `sweep` — angular gradient around `center`, swept from
  `startAngle` (default `0`) to `endAngle` (default `2π`) in
  radians.

`colors` lists at least two color values; `stops` (optional) places
each color at a position in `[0, 1]`. `tileMode` controls behaviour
past the gradient extents.

blendMode.description: Curated subset of blend modes covering the common tinting use cases (`srcIn` for solid tint, `color` for hue swap, `screen`/`overlay`/`multiply` for atmosphere).

BackgroundImage.description:
Image fill behind a box's content. `image` accepts the same forms as
the `image` widget's `src` (`bundle://`, `https?://`, `data:`,
`client://`). `fit` and `alignment` mirror the `image` widget's
semantics. `opacity` applies an alpha multiplier; `colorFilter`
composites a tint via the named blend mode. `repeat` controls
tiling behaviour outside the image's natural bounds.

duration.description: Total transition duration in milliseconds. Defaults to the M3 motion-system value for the chosen style.

curve.description: Easing curve for the transition's progress. Defaults to `emphasized`.

reverse.description: When true, run the transition in reverse direction (for back navigation).

RouteTransition.description:
Per-route page transition. Declared on a `RouteValue` so every
`navigate` to that route uses the same transition, OR overridden
inline on a `navigate` action. Six canonical styles:

- `slide` — incoming page slides over the outgoing along the
  direction (M3 default for forward navigation).
- `fade` — cross-fade between pages at the same position.
- `scale` — outgoing scales out, incoming scales in.
- `cube` — perspective-rotate as if turning a 3D cube; needs a
  perspective-aware compositor (falls back to `slide` when
  unavailable).
- `sharedAxis` — coordinated slide+fade across an axis (M3 motion
  pattern for sibling-level navigation).
- `fadeThrough` — outgoing fades out while incoming fades in
  after a brief zero-opacity bridge (M3 motion pattern for
  top-level navigation).

indicatorColor.description: Selection indicator pill color (rail / drawer / tabs).

indicatorShape.description: Selection indicator corner radius. Defaults to a fully-rounded pill.

dividerColor.description: Drawer-only — color of dividers between item groups.

dividerIndent.description: Drawer-only — leading inset before each divider.

labelStyle.description: Default text style for item labels (selected and unselected). Per-item override via NavItem.style.labelStyle.

selectedColor.description: Foreground color (icon + label) for the selected item.

NavigationStyle.description:
Visual styling for a navigation surface (drawer / rail / tabs /
bottomBar). Every field is optional — omitted fields fall back to
the active theme's component defaults. Used by
`NavigationConfig.style` (whole surface) and `NavItem.style`
(per-item override).

Backgrounds compose: `backgroundColor` paints first, then
`backgroundImage` paints over it.

oneOf.description: Binding expression that resolves to a TextStyle object — typically `{{theme.typography.<role>}}`.

height.description: Line-height multiplier — `height * fontSize` is the per-line vertical advance.

shader.description: Gradient/shader fill applied to the rendered text via a shader mask. When set, `color` is ignored.

TextStyle.description:
Inline text style applied per `text` widget instance. Layered on
top of an M3 typography role when `variant` is set, or used
standalone. Mirrors the theme `TextStyle` token shape (fontFamily,
fontSize, fontWeight, lineHeight, letterSpacing, fontFeatureSettings)
and adds the per-instance fields a content app needs: color,
decoration, shader (gradient fill), and shadow stack.

BorderSide.description: One side of a box border.

lines.description: Number of body-text lines the cap spans vertically.

glyph.description: Override character. Default: first character of the text widget's `text`.

style.description: Cap-specific style overrides, layered on top of the parent widget's style. `fontSize` is computed from `lines × parent.fontSize` when omitted.

DropCap.description:
Typographic drop-cap ornament for long-form text (book chapters,
magazine articles). The first character of the text widget's content
is rendered enlarged in the top-left corner; the surrounding text
flows in a left-indented column for `lines` lines and then continues
full width below the cap. `glyph` overrides the cap character (e.g.
rendering a stylised dingbat instead of the literal first letter).
`style` layers on top of the parent text widget's resolved style for
cap-specific overrides (typically a different `fontFamily` and
larger `fontWeight`).

Sliver.description:
Scrollable region inside `scrollView.slivers`. Discriminated by
`type`:

- **sliverAppBar** — collapsing parallax masthead at the top of a
  scroll view. Expands to `expandedHeight` then shrinks to a
  fixed bar as the user scrolls. `flexibleSpace` paints the hero
  area; `pinned`/`floating` control whether the bar sticks.
- **sliverPersistentHeader** — section header that stays visible
  while its section's items scroll. `minExtent`/`maxExtent` set
  the size envelope; the runtime morphs the header within it.
- **sliverList** — vertical list with variable-height items.
  Same `items` + `itemTemplate` contract as the `list` widget.
- **sliverGrid** — 2D grid with the same `items` / `columns`
  contract as `grid`.
- **sliverFixedExtentList** — list whose items all share the
  same scroll-axis extent (uniform row height). Faster than
  `sliverList` because the runtime can skip per-item measurement.

Each shape carries the type discriminator on `type` and is only
valid inside a `scrollView.slivers` array. The runtime rejects
these shapes at the top level.

Span.description:
Inline span in a `richText` composition. Discriminated by which
identifying field is present:

- `text` (string) → **TextSpan** — a styled run of text. Optional
  `style: TextStyle`, `onTap: Action`, and nested `children:
  Span[]` for further inline structure.
- `widget` (Widget) → **WidgetSpan** — an inline-widget placeholder
  (icon, image, custom badge) that the rich-text layout treats as
  a single character. `alignment` and `baseline` control its
  vertical placement relative to the surrounding line.

The two shapes are mutually exclusive — a span MUST NOT carry both
`text` and `widget`.

BoxShadow.description:
A drop shadow painted beneath the box. Multiple shadows compose via
`decoration.boxShadow: array<BoxShadow>` and stack from the first
(closest to the box) outward.

BoxBorder.description:
Border around a box. Either a single uniform side applied to all four
edges, or per-side overrides. Per-side keys (`top` / `bottom` /
`left` / `right`) take precedence over `all`.

BoxDecoration.description:
Visual decoration for a box. `color` and `gradient` are mutually
exclusive — when both are present `gradient` wins. `image` paints
beneath the gradient/color (background image layer). `border`,
`borderRadius`, and `boxShadow` apply on top of the fill. `shape`
toggles between rectangle (default) and circle (uses the box's
shorter side as diameter; ignores `borderRadius`). `backdropBlur`
applies a Gaussian blur to whatever is behind the box (for frosted-
glass overlays); the value is the blur sigma.

alertDialog.description:
Modal alert with an optional title, an optional body (string or widget),
and a list of labeled action buttons. Dispatched via the `dialog` action
(`{ "type": "dialog", "dialog": { "type": "alertDialog", ... } }`). The
runtime renders it as a `Dialog` surface scoped to the current navigator
(not the root navigator), so tapping an action pops only the dialog.

actions.description:
Action buttons rendered in the dialog footer. Each entry:
  - `label` (required): button text.
  - `variant`: one of `text` / `elevated` / `outlined` / `filled`; default `text`.
  - `primary`: highlights the button as the default action.
  - `onTap`: the action fired on press. The runtime pops the dialog
    BEFORE executing `onTap`, so handlers that trigger state changes or
    navigation do not race with the dialog dismissal.

icon.description:
Displays an icon. The `icon` property accepts five source forms — pick
the lightest option that fits the asset:

1. **Named (built-in)** — `"home"`, `"folder_open"`, … from the runtime's
   Material Icons resolver table. Zero-cost.
2. **Codepoint** — `{codepoint, fontFamily?, fontPackage?}` for Material
   Icons codepoints not in the name table.
3. **URL** — `https://…` or `http://…` (raster or SVG). Fetched and cached
   at first render.
4. **Bundle SVG asset** — `assets/icons/<name>.svg` resolved from the bundle
   asset map. Used for app-specific icon sets shipped with the bundle.
5. **Local SVG inline** — `data:image/svg+xml;…` data URI for one-off icons
   embedded directly in the DSL.

The optional `shader` field paints the icon with a gradient/shader fill
(rather than a solid `color`). When both are set the shader wins.

`Tooltip` surface.

Responsive icon-size token. Equivalent to passing the same string
via `size`; resolves through `AppIconSizes.of(context)` and tracks
the form-factor scale (compact / medium / desktop / embedded).

color.description:
Icon color. Applied to named / codepoint / SVG forms; for raster
`URL` form it tints when supported. Ignored when `shader` is set.

shader.description:
Gradient/shader fill (linear / radial / sweep). Painted via a
shader mask over the icon's glyph alpha. Mutually exclusive with
`color` — when both are present `shader` wins.

imageFilter.description:
Wraps a child with a colour / blur filter. Distinct from
`BoxDecoration.image.colorFilter` (which colours an image
background) — `imageFilter` filters the entire rendered subtree.
Use for sepia/grayscale book pages, blur-on-hover thumbnails,
saturation-bumped hero shots.

filter.description:
Filter kind:
- `sepia` — warm-tone tint mimicking aged photograph.
- `grayscale` — desaturate to luminance.
- `blur` — Gaussian blur (sigma controlled by `intensity`).
- `saturation` — multiply chroma by `intensity` (0 = grayscale, 1 = unchanged, >1 = pop).
- `brightness` — multiply luminance.
- `contrast` — increase / decrease contrast.
- `invert` — colour invert (negative).

intensity.description:
Filter strength. Range and default depend on `filter`:
- `sepia` / `grayscale` / `invert` — `0.0` (off) .. `1.0` (full). Default `1.0`.
- `blur` — sigma in pixels (`0.0` off, typical 4..16).
- `saturation` / `brightness` / `contrast` — multiplier (`1.0` unchanged).

anyOf.description:
Filter strength. Range and default depend on `filter`:
- `sepia` / `grayscale` / `invert` — `0.0` (off) .. `1.0` (full). Default `1.0`.
- `blur` — sigma in pixels (`0.0` off, typical 4..16).
- `saturation` / `brightness` / `contrast` — multiplier (`1.0` unchanged).

(articles, book chapters, captions with inline emphasis or icons).
Each `Span` is either a styled text run or an inline widget; the
paragraph-level `style` provides the base TextStyle that individual

rive.description:
Plays a Rive animation (`.riv` asset) — the alternative to
`lottieAnimation` when the asset was authored in Rive. State-machine
inputs are driven via `inputs` so the same asset reacts to host
state changes (toggle, scrub, trigger). Distinct from Lottie:
Rive supports interactive state machines, vector deformation, and
smaller binary footprints; Lottie is After Effects-based and
better suited to time-driven motion graphics.

scrollAnimated.description:
Animation driven by scroll position rather than time. Wraps a
child and reads from an ancestor scroll view's offset. Each
`binding` maps a scroll-offset window to a property tween — when
scroll passes through `[fromOffset, toOffset]` the property
interpolates between `fromValue` and `toValue` linearly (or via
`curve`).

Common uses: parallax masthead (image translates 50% scroll
speed), reveal-on-scroll (fade in as element enters viewport),
shrinking title (font scales as user scrolls down).

bindings.description:
Per-property scroll bindings. Each entry is
`{property, fromOffset, toOffset, fromValue, toValue, curve?}`:
- `property` — `opacity` / `scale` / `translateX` /
  `translateY` / `rotate`. Other properties may be wired by
  runtime extensions.
- `fromOffset` / `toOffset` — scroll-position window in
  logical pixels.
- `fromValue` / `toValue` — interpolated target values for the
  property at the window endpoints.
- `curve` — optional easing across the window. Defaults to
  `linear`.

scrollBar.description:
Wraps a scrollable child with a visible scroll bar.

The runtime MUST provide a shared scroll controller to both the scroll bar and its scrollable child. When `thumbVisibility` or `trackVisibility` is `true`, the controller is required to drive the visibility; omitting it is a runtime error. Authors SHOULD NOT manage the controller manually — the runtime injects it transparently so the child scrollable (`scrollView`, `singleChildScrollView`, `list`, `grid`, `pageView`) binds to the same position.

scrollView.description:
Scrollable viewport. Two layout modes — pick one per instance:

- **Linear mode** — `child` (single widget) or `children` (wrapped
  in an implicit linear column along `direction`). Simplest for
  everyday scrolling content.
- **Sliver mode** — `slivers` array of `Sliver` primitives. Use
  for collapsing app bars, sticky section headers, parallax
  mastheads, or mixing list/grid sections in one viewport.

The three input slots (`child`, `children`, `slivers`) are
mutually exclusive.
